Charged phosphate-based positive electrode materials such as Lix-
FePO4 [9–11] and Li3xV2(PO4)3 [12,13] do not react strongly with
electrolyte at temperatures up to 350 C, but have comparatively
small volumetric energy density. The existence of stable P–O bonds
is believed to be one reason for the ‘‘inertness” of the phosphate-
based positive electrode materials.
Recently, a new framework material based on the phosphate
polyanion, LiVPO4F, has been proposed by Barker et al. [14–16].
